target.title: fix(ui): adopt inFlightRun on Control UI history reload
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Control UI loses the in-progress assistant response when the user navigates away from a running chat session and returns; only the last committed user message is shown even though the gateway run is still active.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90755: session reconnect state drifts from the gateway's active run in the web UI, causing user-visible message loss and idle appearance during live runs.

What is the intended outcome?

- Control UI adopts the existing `chat.history` `inFlightRun` contract (already used by TUI) on history reload, rehydrating `chatRunId` / `chatStream` so partial assistant text or a reading indicator appears after switch-back.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No gateway contract changes, no active tool-call replay extension, no CHANGELOG edit, no unrelated UI refactors.

What does success look like?

- After `loadChatHistory`, when the gateway reports an in-flight run, the Control UI shows buffered assistant text or a reading indicator and continues receiving live deltas for that run.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` (`adoptInFlightRunFromChatHistory` + call site after stream reset)
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` (`loadChatHistory inFlightRun adoption`)

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90755

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90755 (ClawSweeper queueable-fix / fix-shape-clear / source-repro)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- ClawSweeper marked queueable with Best possible solution: adopt existing `inFlightRun` in Control UI, mirror TUI.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI ignores `inFlightRun` from `chat.history` on session switch-back.
- Real environment tested: Local checkout on `fix/issue-90755-inflight-run`; Playwright + Edge driving Control UI E2E harness (`startControlUiE2eServer`) with mock Gateway `chat.history` returning `inFlightRun`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
export PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH="/c/Program Files (x86)/Microsoft/Edge/Application/msedge.exe"
node --import tsx scripts/capture-proof-90755.mts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
```

Browser repro steps (mock gateway, real Control UI DOM):
1. Open Session A — only committed user message visible (`01-chat-before-switch-back.png`).
2. Switch to Session B (clears local stream state).
3. Switch back to Session A; deferred `chat.history` resolves with `inFlightRun: { runId: "run-proof-90755", text: "still working in the background…" }`.
4. Control UI shows streaming assistant bubble + in-progress indicator (`02-chat-after-inflight-restore.png`).

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "chatRunId": "run-proof-90755",
  "chatStream": "still working in the background…",
  "visibleInDom": "still working in the background…"
}
```

Screenshots (committed under `proof/pr-90755/` on this branch):
- Before switch-back: `proof/pr-90755/01-chat-before-switch-back.png`
- After `inFlightRun` adoption: `proof/pr-90755/02-chat-after-inflight-restore.png`

- Observed result after fix: After session switch-back, Control UI renders buffered assistant text (`still working in the background…`) and shows the in-progress/streaming state instead of appearing idle with only the user message.
- What was not tested: Live gateway + real agent streaming run; active tool-call replay mid-run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Mock Gateway supplies `inFlightRun` (same contract as production `chat.history`); browser is local Edge via Playwright. Not reporter's production Control UI instance.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): `01-chat-before-switch-back.png` — Session A after initial load shows only the committed user message, no in-progress assistant bubble.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` — 119 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` — 65 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts` — 104 passed
- `pnpm oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` — pass
- `pnpm check:test-types` — pass

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `loadChatHistory inFlightRun adoption` — buffered text restore + empty-text reading-indicator adoption.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- `chatRunId` stayed null after history reload despite gateway `inFlightRun` payload.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A — regression tests added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Returning to a running session now shows in-progress assistant output instead of appearing idle.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Interaction between history stream reset and in-flight adoption when local stream state already exists.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Adoption runs after existing reset logic; existing orphaned-stream materialization tests unchanged; new focused adoption tests added.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Real browser proof added (`proof/pr-90755/`). Await ClawSweeper re-review / maintainer merge.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Optional live gateway proof if maintainer requests; active tool-call replay out of scope unless reported.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- ClawSweeper `status: needs proof` — added Control UI browser screenshots + Playwright capture (`scripts/capture-proof-90755.mts`, local-only).
